coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy 7–8 miles at Z2 (130–140 bpm), rolling terrain, no hard efforts. You're in week 21 taper on a Wednesday, which normally calls for vert work—but your recovery score is 62 (moderate) and sleep last night was only 6.18h. The goal now is retention of sharpness without additional fatigue debt. Dial back the intensity you showed on Sunday (12mi@134bpm) and Monday (6mi@130bpm). This is a confidence run: dial into sub-threshold cruising, feel the legs come alive under minimal stress, and bank the adaptation your peak phase already built. Save the alpine-grade hill repeats for Saturday if you're genuinely fresh.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Eat 60–80g carbs + 15–20g protein in the 2 hours before the run; aim for 30–40g carbs + electrolytes during if you go beyond 90 minutes. Post-run: 40g carbs + 25g protein within 30 minutes to start glycogen repletion and cap off a lighter week. You're eating into a taper, so don't under-fuel out of fear of gaining weight—your body needs fuel to consolidate the VO₂ and vert adaptation from peak phase. Skip the energy gels unless you hit 2+ hours; this is a steady-state run, not a race simulation.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Sleep is the leak in your system right now: 6.18h last night is 1.5+ hours below your 30-day average (4.83h is alarming—confirm that number). Prioritize 8 hours tonight. You're 93 days out from the Alps; travel, work stress, and ambient training load are all eroding recovery. Use ice baths or contrast therapy only if they don't disrupt sleep; otherwise, skip them this week. Elevate your feet for 10–15 minutes post-run, hydrate 500ml+ water in the next 2 hours, and defer any strength work until Friday at earliest.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: Taper anxiety is real at this distance and timeline. You're cutting volume hard (6.0mi this week vs. 45.1 last week) and the temptation is to prove fitness by hammering Wednesday or Thursday. Resist it. Your peak phase was solid—12mi at 134bpm, consistent vert work. The Julian Alps 50K will demand alpine judgment and pacing discipline more than raw speed. Use these 13 weeks to dial your nutrition strategy (fuel gels, salt, water intake on sparse aid stations), mentally rehearse the 2,500m descent (quadriceps tendon stress), and trust the work. One confident, easy run today beats a blown taper every time.
